ai_summary: Elon Musk announces that Tesla's Cybercab has begun production, marking a major milestone in the company's autonomous vehicle strategy. This represents a critical scaling transition from prototype to manufacturing for Tesla's robotaxi product line, demonstrating progress on one of the company's key long-term growth initiatives in autonomous driving and fleet electrification.
embed_html:
Cybercab has started production pic.twitter.com/MAeswanf96

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 24, 2026

text: Grok Voice is used by @Starlink https://t.co/3PEzU0krpU
ai_summary: Elon Musk announces that Grok Voice (xAI's conversational AI product) is being deployed and used by Starlink, demonstrating cross-portfolio integration of his companies' technologies. This shows a practical business application of xAI's Grok AI assistant within Starlink's operations, illustrating how internal AI capabilities can be leveraged across different business units for operational efficiency.
embed_html:
Grok Voice is used by @Starlink https://t.co/3PEzU0krpU

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 24, 2026

text:
@yunta_tsai Tesla AI is where the rubber hits the road. “Make no mistakes” is not just a meme joke for self-driving AI. 

Tesla AI saves lives every day, at large scale. No other AI team can yet make that claim.
summary: @yunta_tsai Tesla AI is where the rubber hits the road. “Make no mistakes” is not just a meme joke for self-driving AI. Tesla AI saves lives every day, at large scale. No other AI team can yet make that claim.

parent.text: A lot of AI engineers have spent their most productive years chasing benchmarks instead of solving useful cases they can relate to. If the car fails to take me home, then I simply fail at my job and can’t go home. That’s it.
parent.name: Yun-Ta Tsai
parent.embed_html:
A lot of AI engineers have spent their most productive years chasing benchmarks instead of solving useful cases they can relate to.

If the car fails to take me home, then I simply fail at my job and can’t go home. That’s it.

— Yun-Ta Tsai (@yunta_tsai) April 24, 2026

ai_summary: The thread discusses a critical distinction in AI engineering priorities: Tesla's AI team focuses on real-world, high-stakes applications (autonomous driving) where failure has direct consequences, rather than chasing benchmark metrics disconnected from practical problems. The business insight is that outcome-driven engineering—where the product must work reliably in production to succeed—creates fundamentally different incentives and accountability than benchmark optimization, and Tesla claims to be the only AI team currently deploying life-saving AI at scale in vehicles.
embed_html:
Tesla AI is where the rubber hits the road. “Make no mistakes” is not just a meme joke for self-driving AI.

Tesla AI saves lives every day, at large scale. No other AI team can yet make that claim.

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 24, 2026
